summary:
The ophthalmic artery does not arise from the external carotid artery but instead predominantly branches off the internal carotid artery. Despite rare anomalous origins, its classification as a non-branch of the external carotid artery aligns with standard anatomy and prevailing medical education.  

\boxed{D}
